    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U ${DB_USER:-dealflow} -d ${DB_NAME:-dealflow}"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 10

  gateway:
    build: ./backend/gateway
    container_name: dqplus-gateway
    environment:
      PORT: 3000
      HOST: 0.0.0.0
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_PORT: 5432
      DB_NAME: ${DB_NAME:-dealflow}
      DB_USER: ${DB_USER:-dealflow}
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:[REDACTED]}
      DB_SSL: "false"
      JWT_SECRET: ${JWT_SECRET:[REDACTED]}
      JWT_EXPIRES_IN: 1d
      EXTRACT_SERVICE_URL: http://extract:3003
      MATCHING_API_URL: http://ai-data-platform:8000
    ports:
      - "${GATEWAY_PORT:-3000}:3000"
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "python3 -c \"import urllib.request; urllib.request.urlopen('http://localhost:3000/health')\" || exit 1"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  matching-engine:
    build: ./backend/matching-engine
    container_name: dqplus-matching-engine
    environment:
      PORT: 3002
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_PORT: 5432
      DB_NAME: ${DB_NAME:-dealflow}
      DB_USER: ${DB_USER:-dealflow}
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:[REDACTED]}
      DB_SSL: "false"
      MATCH_VECTOR_WEIGHT: ${MATCH_VECTOR_WEIGHT:-0.7}
      MATCH_ATTR_WEIGHT: ${MATCH_ATTR_WEIGHT:-0.3}
      MATCH_CANDIDATE_POOL: ${MATCH_CANDIDATE_POOL:-50}
      MATCHING_API_URL: http://ai-data-platform:8000
    ports:
      - "${MATCHING_PORT:-3002}:3002"
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "python3 -c \"import urllib.request; urllib.request.urlopen('http://localhost:3002/health')\" || exit 1"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  extract:
    build: ./backend/agent/extract
    container_name: dqplus-extract
    environment:
      PORT: 3003
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_PORT: 5432
      DB_NAME: ${DB_NAME:-dealflow}
      DB_USER: ${DB_USER:-dealflow}
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:[REDACTED]}
      DB_SSL: "false"
      # Empty key → deterministic feature-hash embedding fallback (no semantic
      # embeddings, but the profile → match flow still works).
      OPENAI_API_KEY: ${OPENAI_API_KEY:-}
      OPENAI_BASE_URL: ${OPENAI_BASE_URL:-https://mkp-api.fptcloud.com}
      OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL: ${OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL:-gpt-4o-mini}
      OPENAI_EMBEDDING_MODEL: ${OPENAI_EMBEDDING_MODEL:-text-embedding-3-small}
      EMBEDDING_DIM: ${EMBEDDING_DIM:-1536}
    ports:
      - "${EXTRACT_PORT:-3003}:3003"
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "python3 -c \"import urllib.request; urllib.request.urlopen('http://localhost:3003/health')\" || exit 1"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  ai-data-platform:
    build:
      context: ./ai-data-platform
      dockerfile: api/Dockerfile
    container_name: dqplus-ai-data-platform
    environment:
      DATABASE_URL: postgres://${DB_USER:[REDACTED:db-password]@postgres:5432/${DB_NAME:-dealflow}
      # Same secret as the gateway: the dashboard (GET /) is admin-only and
      # verifies gateway-issued JWTs (role=admin, DB-provisioned).
      JWT_SECRET: ${JWT_SECRET:[REDACTED]}
    ports:
      - "${MATCHING_API_PORT:-8000}:8000"
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy

  # web is internal-only and scalable (no container_name / no published port) so
  # multiple replicas can run side by side during a rolling deploy. The `proxy`
  # service owns the public port and load-balances across replicas.
  web:
    build: ./frontend
    expose:
      - "80"
    depends_on:
      - gateway
      - matching-engine
      - extract
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "wget -q -O /dev/null http://127.0.0.1:80/ || exit 1"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 5s

  proxy:
    image: nginx:1.27-alpine
    container_name: dqplus-proxy
    volumes:
      - ./deploy/proxy.conf:/etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf:ro
    ports:
      - "${WEB_PORT:-5173}:80"
    depends_on:
      - web

volumes:
  pgdata:

content.text: Env is correct (`postgres:5432`) — the crash is a **network-attachment** problem: the first `up` aborted at the port-8000 bind conflict, leaving the container not joined to the compose network, so `postgres` won't resolve. A force-recreate fixes it:

input.command:
cd /home/delus/Documents/code/hackathon/dqplus-hackathon-2026
echo "=== networks on the broken platform container vs gateway ==="
docker inspect dqplus-ai-data-platform --format 'platform nets: {{range $k,$v := .NetworkSettings.Networks}}{{$k}} {{end}}' 2>/dev/null
docker inspect dqplus-gateway --format 'gateway  nets: {{range $k,$v := .NetworkSettings.Networks}}{{$k}} {{end}}' 2>/dev/null
echo
echo "=== force recreate ==="
docker rm -f dqplus-ai-data-platform >/dev/null 2>&1
docker compose up -d --force-recreate ai-data-platform 2>&1 | tail -4
sleep 4
docker ps --format '{{.Names}}\t{{.Status}}\t{{.Ports}}' | grep -i ai-data-platform || echo "NOT RUNNING"

content.text:
All done and verified. Here's the final state.

## Everything is committed, deployed, and live
- **Commit `3cf4158`** (authored by you, already on `origin/master`) contains all the RAG/GraphRAG work, the review + FPT fixes, and the docker-compose rename. No `.env` or API key was committed — secrets stay gitignored. I removed the redundant empty branch I'd created.
- **Service renamed**: `matching-api` → **`ai-data-platform`** (container `dqplus-ai-data-platform`), rebuilt with the new code, running on `:8000`. `gateway` + `matching-engine` recreated to use the new hostname. The old `dqplus-matching-api` is gone. Public web path untouched.
- **Key wired** scoped to just this service (via `env_file: ai-data-platform/.env`), so `extract` (which needs its 1536 column) is unaffected.

## Verified live on the running container
- **RAG** — `POST /matches/founders/{id}/investors` returns real FPT-embedded matches: agritech/seed founder → Ascend Vietnam Ventures, FPT Ventures, ThinkZone, VIISA, East Ventures, with vector + attribute reasons.
- **GraphRAG** — `GET /entities/{id}/graph-matches` works (e.g. `startup:alterno` → 1 match). Confirmed the low hit-rate is **genuine data sparsity**, not a bug: all 683 edges' `dst_id`s are unresolved `name:...` placeholders pointing to companies outside the 115-entity seed, so the slug re-resolution only connects a few — exactly as the module documents.
- **Corpus**: 115/115 entities embedded (FPT `multilingual-e5-large`, 1024-dim).

## The three things your key exposed (now fixed in the committed code)
1. FPT is behind **Cloudflare** — every call needs a browser User-Agent (was getting `error 1010`).
2. FPT hosts `multilingual-e5-large` / `Vietnamese_Embedding`, **not** `text-embedding-3-small`.
3. Those are **1024-dim** — column + `EMBEDDING_DIM` moved from 1536 to 1024.

## One thing worth knowing
Query-time embedding makes a live FPT call per request (I hit one transient SSL timeout that a retry cleared). If you want it bulletproof under load, I can add a small retry/backoff around `embed_text` — it currently raises on failure (which is correct: it must **not** silently fall back to hash vectors, or queries wouldn't match the FPT corpus).

Want me to add that retry, or switch the embedder to `Vietnamese_Embedding` and compare match quality?

content.text:
Found it. **No — the public interface still points at the old engine, by design of the routing.** Here's the exact chain for your curl:

`https://dqplus.ddns.net/api/matches/founders/{uuid}/investors`
→ `frontend/nginx.conf:22` → `proxy_pass http://matching-engine:3002/matches/...`
→ the **old** matching-engine, which queries `extracted_profiles` by that user UUID.

The new platform RAG endpoint is **not on this path at all**, and there's a deeper contract mismatch. Let me verify the identity/data situation before I lay out the fix:

input.command:
cd /home/delus/Documents/code/hackathon/dqplus-hackathon-2026
UID=d9ca5ece-89a8-4ef5-9728-0a48e6fe2630
echo "=== is that UUID a gateway user with an extracted_profile? ==="
docker exec dqplus-postgres psql -U dealflow -d dealflow -tA -c "
SELECT 'extracted_profiles rows for user='||count(*) FROM extracted_profiles WHERE user_id='$UID';
SELECT 'total extracted_profiles='||count(*) FROM extracted_profiles;
SELECT 'entities (platform corpus, slug ids)='||count(*) FROM entities;"
echo
echo "=== the platform corpus is keyed by SLUGS, not user UUIDs: ==="
docker exec dqplus-postgres psql -U dealflow -d dealflow -tA -c "SELECT id FROM entities WHERE type='investor' LIMIT 3;"
echo
echo "=== new platform GET route present? (it is POST-with-body only) ==="
docker exec dqplus-ai-data-platform sh -c "grep -oE '@app\.(get|post)\(\"/matches[^\"]*\"' /app/api/app.py" 2>/dev/null || echo "(cannot read)"

content.text:
Confirmed the full picture. **No — nothing changed on the public interface.** Your curl still hits the old engine, and there are three reasons the new platform endpoint can't serve that request as-is:

**1. Routing** — `frontend/nginx.conf:22` sends `/api/matches/...` → `matching-engine:3002`. The platform (`:8000`) isn't on that path.

**2. Method + payload** — your call is `GET ...?limit=50` with no body. The platform endpoint I built is `POST /matches/founders/{id}/investors` with the profile **in the request body** (that was the "caller passes profile in body" choice). A GET with no body won't match it.

**3. Identity + corpus** — the path id `d9ca5ece-…` is a **gateway user UUID** (it has 1 row in `extracted_profiles`, out of 7 users). The old engine matches that user against the other 6 *users*. The platform is keyed by **entity slugs** (`investor:do-ventures`) and matches against the **115 curated entities**, not users. Different ids, different result set.

So what you're seeing is the old user-vs-user matching, untouched.

### To actually serve the platform on this URL
This circles back to your original ask ("the platform must get the profile from backend"). The body-based POST was a detour — the real client is GET-by-UUID. The clean cutover:

1. **Platform**: add `GET /matches/founders/{user_id}/investors` (+ the reverse) that **self-fetches** the user's `embedding_text`/attributes from `extracted_profiles` by UUID (shared DB), embeds it with e5-1024, and RAGs over the entity corpus — returning the old-compatible shape.
2. **nginx** (`frontend/nginx.conf`): repoint `location /api/matches` → `http://ai-data-platform:8000/matches`. This removes `matching-engine` from the matches path entirely (bare `/matches` already just proxied to the platform anyway) — matching your "from now on we work with the ai-data-platform."
3. **Deploy**: rebuild the platform image, then **rolling-deploy `web`** (the nginx config is baked into that image — must use `bash deploy/rolling-deploy.sh`, never `up -d --build web`).

**One consequence to accept:** matches become **curated investors/partners** (entity slugs + names), not the 7 registered users. That's inherent to using the platform's corpus — but the frontend match cards currently key off `userId`, so they may need a small tweak to render entity matches.

Want me to implement this cutover (new GET endpoints → repoint nginx → rolling deploy)? And should the founders route return **investors only** (faithful to the URL) or **all partner types** (the platform's fuller corpus — corporations/universities too)?

"""EmbeddingMatcher — pgvector RAG match (spec §12 drop-in for the Matcher port).

A requester profile is embedded once (embedding.embed_text) and KNN-searched against the
`entities.embedding` corpus, then re-scored with a pure attribute score. The composite
mirrors the matching-engine: ``vector_weight * vscore + attr_weight * attr`` (0.7 / 0.3).

`attribute_score` is a standalone PURE function (no I/O) so the rules are unit-testable
without a DB. It mirrors matching-engine/scoring.py weights — sector Jaccard 0.4, stage
0.3, geo/region 0.2, check-size fit 0.1 / 0.05 — over the entity `profile.normalized`
shape, and each firing rule appends a human-readable reason.

asyncpg has no vector adapter, so the query embedding is passed as a pgvector literal
string and cast `$1::vector` in SQL.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
import json
import os
import urllib.request

from . import normalize_sectors
from ..embedding import embed_text, entity_text

OPENAI_API_KEY = [REDACTED]("OPENAI_API_KEY") or ""
OPENAI_BASE_URL = os.environ.get("OPENAI_BASE_URL") or "https://mkp-api.fptcloud.com"
OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL = os.environ.get("OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL") or "gemma-4-31B-it"


# ── Pure attribute scoring (no I/O, unit-testable) ─────────────────────────────────
def attribute_score(query_norm: dict, cand_norm: dict) -> tuple[float, list[str]]:
    """Score a candidate's `normalized` profile against the requester's. Returns
    (score in [0, 1], reasons). Mirrors matching-engine/scoring.py weights."""
    query_norm = query_norm or {}
    cand_norm = cand_norm or {}
    reasons: list[str] = []
    score = 0.0

    # sector overlap (Jaccard, bilingual-normalized) — weight 0.4
    q_sectors = normalize_sectors(query_norm.get("sectors"))
    c_sectors = normalize_sectors(cand_norm.get("sectors"))
    if q_sectors and c_sectors:
        overlap = q_sectors & c_sectors
        if overlap:
            score += 0.4 * (len(overlap) / len(q_sectors | c_sectors))
            reasons.append(f"sector overlap: {', '.join(sorted(overlap))}")

    # stage match — weight 0.3
    q_stage = str(query_norm.get("stage") or "").lower().strip()
    c_stages = _lower(_as_list(cand_norm.get("stages") or cand_norm.get("stage")))
    if q_stage and q_stage in c_stages:
        score += 0.3
        reasons.append(f"stage match: {q_stage}")

    # geo / region match — weight 0.2
    q_regions = set(_lower(_regions(query_norm)))
    c_geos = set(_lower(_regions(cand_norm)))
    if "global" in c_geos and q_regions:
        score += 0.2
        reasons.append("partner operates globally")
    else:
        geo_overlap = q_regions & c_geos
        if geo_overlap:
            score += 0.2
            reasons.append(f"geography match: {', '.join(sorted(geo_overlap))}")

    # check-size fit — weight 0.1 (both bounds) / 0.05 (one bound)
    ask = _num(query_norm.get("funding_ask_usd") or query_norm.get("check_size_usd"))
    min_check = _num(cand_norm.get("check_size_min_usd"))
    max_check = _num(cand_norm.get("check_size_max_usd"))
    if ask is not None and (min_check is not None or max_check is not None):
        above_min = min_check is None or ask >= min_check
        below_max = max_check is None or ask <= max_check
        if above_min and below_max:
            score += 0.1 if (min_check is not None and max_check is not None) else 0.05
            reasons.append("check size fits funding ask")

    return min(score, 1.0), reasons


# ── pgvector RAG match ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
async def match(pool, *, query_profile: dict, target_types: list[str], limit: int,
                filters: dict | None = None, rerank: bool = False,
                vector_weight: float = 0.7, attr_weight: float = 0.3) -> dict:
    query_norm = _normalized(query_profile)
    vec = await embed_text(entity_text(query_profile))
    vec_literal = "[" + ",".join(str(x) for x in vec) + "]"

    conditions = ["type = ANY($2)", "embedding IS NOT NULL"]
    params: list = [vec_literal, target_types]
    for key, value in (filters or {}).items():
        params.append(json.dumps({key: value}))
        conditions.append(f"profile->'normalized' @> ${len(params)}::jsonb")

    candidate_pool = max(50, limit)
    query = f"""
        SELECT id, type, name, profile, 1 - (embedding <=> $1::vector) AS vscore
        FROM entities
        WHERE {' AND '.join(conditions)}
        ORDER BY embedding <=> $1::vector
        LIMIT {candidate_pool}
    """
    rows = await pool.fetch(query, *params)

    matches = []
    for r in rows:
        profile = r["profile"]
        if isinstance(profile, str):
            profile = json.loads(profile) if profile else {}
        vscore = float(r["vscore"])
        attr, reasons = attribute_score(query_norm, (profile or {}).get("normalized") or {})
        composite = vector_weight * vscore + attr_weight * attr
        matches.append({
            "id": r["id"],
            "name": r["name"],
            "type": r["type"],
            "score": round(composite, 4),
            "vectorScore": round(vscore, 4),
            "attributeScore": round(attr, 4),
            "reasons": reasons,
            "profile": profile or {},
        })

    matches.sort(key=lambda m: m["score"], reverse=True)
    matches = matches[:limit]

    if rerank and matches:
        await _rerank(query_norm, matches)

    return {"matches": matches}


# ── Optional LLM rerank (cited rationale) ──────────────────────────────────────────
async def _rerank(query_norm: dict, matches: list[dict]) -> None:
    """Attach cited rationale_en/rationale_vi to `matches` in place via one FPT chat
    call. On ANY failure (no key, network, bad JSON) the rationales are omitted silently."""
    if not OPENAI_API_KEY:
        return
    try:
        data = await asyncio.to_thread(_chat_rerank, query_norm, matches)
        by_id = {m["id"]: m for m in matches}
        for item in data.get("items") or []:
            m = by_id.get(item.get("id"))
            if m is None:
                continue
            if item.get("rationale_en"):
                m["rationale_en"] = str(item["rationale_en"])
            if item.get("rationale_vi"):
                m["rationale_vi"] = str(item["rationale_vi"])
    except Exception:
        pass


def _chat_rerank(query_norm: dict, matches: list[dict]) -> dict:
    requester = {
        "sectors": sorted(normalize_sectors(query_norm.get("sectors"))),
        "stage": query_norm.get("stage"),
        "looking_for": query_norm.get("looking_for"),
        "description_en": query_norm.get("description_en"),
    }
    candidates = [
        {
            "id": m["id"],
            "name": m["name"],
            "type": m["type"],
            "reasons": m["reasons"],
            "normalized": (m["profile"] or {}).get("normalized") or {},
        }
        for m in matches
    ]
    prompt = (
        "Explain why each PARTNER fits this REQUESTER for a Vietnamese innovation "
        "introduction. Ground every claim ONLY in the data below — invent nothing.\n\n"
        f"REQUESTER\n{json.dumps(requester, ensure_ascii=False)}\n\n"
        f"PARTNERS\n{json.dumps(candidates, ensure_ascii=False)}\n\n"
        "Reply with ONLY valid JSON: {\"items\": [{\"id\": \"...\", \"rationale_en\": "
        "\"1-2 sentences on the concrete fit\", \"rationale_vi\": \"same in Vietnamese\"}]}. "
        "One object per partner above."
    )
    payload = json.dumps({
        "model": OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL,
        "messages": [
            {"role": "system", "content": "You are a match analyst. Reply with valid JSON only."},
            {"role": "user", "content": prompt},
        ],
        "temperature": 0.2,
        "max_tokens": 1024,
    }).encode()
    req = urllib.request.Request(
        f"{OPENAI_BASE_URL}/chat/completions",
        data=payload,
        headers={
            "Content-Type": "application/json",
            "Authorization": f"Bearer {OPENAI_API_KEY}",
            # FPT sits behind Cloudflare, which 403s the default urllib User-Agent.
            "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36 "
                          "(KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/124.0 Safari/537.36",
        },
    )
    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=30) as resp:
        result = json.loads(resp.read().decode())
    return json.loads(result["choices"][0]["message"]["content"])


# ── helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
def _normalized(profile: dict) -> dict:
    """Extract the `normalized` dict from a full entity, a {"profile": {...}} wrapper,
    or a bare normalized dict."""
    profile = profile or {}
    prof = profile.get("profile")
    if isinstance(prof, dict):
        inner = prof.get("normalized")
        return inner if isinstance(inner, dict) else prof
    inner = profile.get("normalized")
    if isinstance(inner, dict):
        return inner
    return profile


def _as_list(v) -> list:
    if v is None:
        return []
    return list(v) if isinstance(v, (list, tuple, set)) else [v]


def _lower(values) -> list[str]:
    return [str(v).lower().strip() for v in values if v is not None and str(v).strip()]


def _regions(norm: dict) -> list:
    out: list = []
    for key in ("target_regions", "geographies", "regions", "geo", "region", "location"):
        v = norm.get(key)
        if isinstance(v, (list, tuple, set)):
            out += list(v)
        elif v:
            out.append(v)
    return out


def _num(v) -> float | None:
    try:
        return None if v is None else float(v)
    except (TypeError, ValueError):
        return None

toolUseResult.originalFile:
"""EmbeddingMatcher — pgvector RAG match (spec §12 drop-in for the Matcher port).

A requester profile is embedded once (embedding.embed_text) and KNN-searched against the
`entities.embedding` corpus, then re-scored with a pure attribute score. The composite
mirrors the matching-engine: ``vector_weight * vscore + attr_weight * attr`` (0.7 / 0.3).

`attribute_score` is a standalone PURE function (no I/O) so the rules are unit-testable
without a DB. It mirrors matching-engine/scoring.py weights — sector Jaccard 0.4, stage
0.3, geo/region 0.2, check-size fit 0.1 / 0.05 — over the entity `profile.normalized`
shape, and each firing rule appends a human-readable reason.

asyncpg has no vector adapter, so the query embedding is passed as a pgvector literal
string and cast `$1::vector` in SQL.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
import json
import os
import urllib.request

from . import normalize_sectors
from ..embedding import embed_text, entity_text

OPENAI_API_KEY = [REDACTED]("OPENAI_API_KEY") or ""
OPENAI_BASE_URL = os.environ.get("OPENAI_BASE_URL") or "https://mkp-api.fptcloud.com"
OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL = os.environ.get("OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL") or "gemma-4-31B-it"


# ── Pure attribute scoring (no I/O, unit-testable) ─────────────────────────────────
def attribute_score(query_norm: dict, cand_norm: dict) -> tuple[float, list[str]]:
    """Score a candidate's `normalized` profile against the requester's. Returns
    (score in [0, 1], reasons). Mirrors matching-engine/scoring.py weights."""
    query_norm = query_norm or {}
    cand_norm = cand_norm or {}
    reasons: list[str] = []
    score = 0.0

    # sector overlap (Jaccard, bilingual-normalized) — weight 0.4
    q_sectors = normalize_sectors(query_norm.get("sectors"))
    c_sectors = normalize_sectors(cand_norm.get("sectors"))
    if q_sectors and c_sectors:
        overlap = q_sectors & c_sectors
        if overlap:
            score += 0.4 * (len(overlap) / len(q_sectors | c_sectors))
            reasons.append(f"sector overlap: {', '.join(sorted(overlap))}")

    # stage match — weight 0.3
    q_stage = str(query_norm.get("stage") or "").lower().strip()
    c_stages = _lower(_as_list(cand_norm.get("stages") or cand_norm.get("stage")))
    if q_stage and q_stage in c_stages:
        score += 0.3
        reasons.append(f"stage match: {q_stage}")

    # geo / region match — weight 0.2
    q_regions = set(_lower(_regions(query_norm)))
    c_geos = set(_lower(_regions(cand_norm)))
    if "global" in c_geos and q_regions:
        score += 0.2
        reasons.append("partner operates globally")
    else:
        geo_overlap = q_regions & c_geos
        if geo_overlap:
            score += 0.2
            reasons.append(f"geography match: {', '.join(sorted(geo_overlap))}")

    # check-size fit — weight 0.1 (both bounds) / 0.05 (one bound)
    ask = _num(query_norm.get("funding_ask_usd") or query_norm.get("check_size_usd"))
    min_check = _num(cand_norm.get("check_size_min_usd"))
    max_check = _num(cand_norm.get("check_size_max_usd"))
    if ask is not None and (min_check is not None or max_check is not None):
        above_min = min_check is None or ask >= min_check
        below_max = max_check is None or ask <= max_check
        if above_min and below_max:
            score += 0.1 if (min_check is not None and max_check is not None) else 0.05
            reasons.append("check size fits funding ask")

    return min(score, 1.0), reasons


# ── pgvector RAG match ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
async def match(pool, *, query_profile: dict, target_types: list[str], limit: int,
                filters: dict | None = None, rerank: bool = False,
                vector_weight: float = 0.7, attr_weight: float = 0.3) -> dict:
    query_norm = _normalized(query_profile)
    vec = await embed_text(entity_text(query_profile))
    vec_literal = "[" + ",".join(str(x) for x in vec) + "]"

    conditions = ["type = ANY($2)", "embedding IS NOT NULL"]
    params: list = [vec_literal, target_types]
    for key, value in (filters or {}).items():
        params.append(json.dumps({key: value}))
        conditions.append(f"profile->'normalized' @> ${len(params)}::jsonb")

    candidate_pool = max(50, limit)
    query = f"""
        SELECT id, type, name, profile, 1 - (embedding <=> $1::vector) AS vscore
        FROM entities
        WHERE {' AND '.join(conditions)}
        ORDER BY embedding <=> $1::vector
        LIMIT {candidate_pool}
    """
    rows = await pool.fetch(query, *params)

    matches = []
    for r in rows:
        profile = r["profile"]
        if isinstance(profile, str):
            profile = json.loads(profile) if profile else {}
        vscore = float(r["vscore"])
        attr, reasons = attribute_score(query_norm, (profile or {}).get("normalized") or {})
        composite = vector_weight * vscore + attr_weight * attr
        matches.append({
            "id": r["id"],
            "name": r["name"],
            "type": r["type"],
            "score": round(composite, 4),
            "vectorScore": round(vscore, 4),
            "attributeScore": round(attr, 4),
            "reasons": reasons,
            "profile": profile or {},
        })

    matches.sort(key=lambda m: m["score"], reverse=True)
    matches = matches[:limit]

    if rerank and matches:
        await _rerank(query_norm, matches)

    return {"matches": matches}


# ── Optional LLM rerank (cited rationale) ──────────────────────────────────────────
async def _rerank(query_norm: dict, matches: list[dict]) -> None:
    """Attach cited rationale_en/rationale_vi to `matches` in place via one FPT chat
    call. On ANY failure (no key, network, bad JSON) the rationales are omitted silently."""
    if not OPENAI_API_KEY:
        return
    try:
        data = await asyncio.to_thread(_chat_rerank, query_norm, matches)
        by_id = {m["id"]: m for m in matches}
        for item in data.get("items") or []:
            m = by_id.get(item.get("id"))
            if m is None:
                continue
            if item.get("rationale_en"):
                m["rationale_en"] = str(item["rationale_en"])
            if item.get("rationale_vi"):
                m["rationale_vi"] = str(item["rationale_vi"])
    except Exception:
        pass


def _chat_rerank(query_norm: dict, matches: list[dict]) -> dict:
    requester = {
        "sectors": sorted(normalize_sectors(query_norm.get("sectors"))),
        "stage": query_norm.get("stage"),
        "looking_for": query_norm.get("looking_for"),
        "description_en": query_norm.get("description_en"),
    }
    candidates = [
        {
            "id": m["id"],
            "name": m["name"],
            "type": m["type"],
            "reasons": m["reasons"],
            "normalized": (m["profile"] or {}).get("normalized") or {},
        }
        for m in matches
    ]
    prompt = (
        "Explain why each PARTNER fits this REQUESTER for a Vietnamese innovation "
        "introduction. Ground every claim ONLY in the data below — invent nothing.\n\n"
        f"REQUESTER\n{json.dumps(requester, ensure_ascii=False)}\n\n"
        f"PARTNERS\n{json.dumps(candidates, ensure_ascii=False)}\n\n"
        "Reply with ONLY valid JSON: {\"items\": [{\"id\": \"...\", \"rationale_en\": "
        "\"1-2 sentences on the concrete fit\", \"rationale_vi\": \"same in Vietnamese\"}]}. "
        "One object per partner above."
    )
    payload = json.dumps({
        "model": OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL,
        "messages": [
            {"role": "system", "content": "You are a match analyst. Reply with valid JSON only."},
            {"role": "user", "content": prompt},
        ],
        "temperature": 0.2,
        "max_tokens": 1024,
    }).encode()
    req = urllib.request.Request(
        f"{OPENAI_BASE_URL}/chat/completions",
        data=payload,
        headers={
            "Content-Type": "application/json",
            "Authorization": f"Bearer {OPENAI_API_KEY}",
            # FPT sits behind Cloudflare, which 403s the default urllib User-Agent.
            "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36 "
                          "(KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/124.0 Safari/537.36",
        },
    )
    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=30) as resp:
        result = json.loads(resp.read().decode())
    return json.loads(result["choices"][0]["message"]["content"])


# ── helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
def _normalized(profile: dict) -> dict:
    """Extract the `normalized` dict from a full entity, a {"profile": {...}} wrapper,
    or a bare normalized dict."""
    profile = profile or {}
    prof = profile.get("profile")
    if isinstance(prof, dict):
        inner = prof.get("normalized")
        return inner if isinstance(inner, dict) else prof
    inner = profile.get("normalized")
    if isinstance(inner, dict):
        return inner
    return profile


def _as_list(v) -> list:
    if v is None:
        return []
    return list(v) if isinstance(v, (list, tuple, set)) else [v]


def _lower(values) -> list[str]:
    return [str(v).lower().strip() for v in values if v is not None and str(v).strip()]


def _regions(norm: dict) -> list:
    out: list = []
    for key in ("target_regions", "geographies", "regions", "geo", "region", "location"):
        v = norm.get(key)
        if isinstance(v, (list, tuple, set)):
            out += list(v)
        elif v:
            out.append(v)
    return out


def _num(v) -> float | None:
    try:
        return None if v is None else float(v)
    except (TypeError, ValueError):
        return None

content.text: Now add the GET-by-UUID endpoints to `app.py` (self-fetch from `extracted_profiles`, 404 preserving the app's extract-then-retry flow):

input.new_string:
return await _rag_match(user_id, "investor", body, ["startup"], types, limit, rerank,
                            sector, stage, region)


# --- GET-by-user variants: the public interface the frontend calls (no body). ------- #
# nginx routes /api/matches/... here (replacing the old matching-engine); the requester's
# profile is self-fetched from extracted_profiles by gateway user id (shared DB).

def _norm_from_attrs(attrs: dict) -> dict:
    """Map an extract-agent attribute set (extracted_profiles.attributes) onto the
    `normalized` keys the matcher's attribute_score reads (founder uses `industry`)."""
    attrs = attrs or {}
    return {
        "sectors": attrs.get("sectors") or attrs.get("industry"),
        "stage": attrs.get("stage"),
        "stages": attrs.get("stages"),
        "target_regions": attrs.get("target_regions"),
        "geographies": attrs.get("geographies"),
        "funding_ask_usd": attrs.get("funding_ask_usd"),
        "check_size_usd": attrs.get("check_size_usd"),
        "looking_for": attrs.get("looking_for"),
        "description_en": attrs.get("product_description") or attrs.get("thesis"),
    }


async def _rag_match_by_user(user_id: str, default_types: list[str], types: str | None,
                             limit: int, rerank: bool) -> dict:
    row = await _pool(app).fetchrow(
        "SELECT role, attributes, embedding_text FROM extracted_profiles WHERE user_id = $1",
        user_id,
    )
    if row is None:
        # Same 404 as the old engine so the app's extract-then-retry flow still triggers.
        raise HTTPException(404, "No extracted profile for user; run extraction first")
    attrs = row["attributes"]
    if isinstance(attrs, str):
        attrs = json.loads(attrs or "{}")
    result = await embedding_matcher.match(
        _pool(app),
        query_text=row["embedding_text"] or "",
        query_norm=_norm_from_attrs(attrs or {}),
        target_types=_target_types(default_types, types),
        limit=limit,
        rerank=rerank,
    )
    return {"userId": user_id, "role": row["role"], "matches": result["matches"]}


@app.get("/matches/founders/{user_id}/investors")
async def matches_founder_to_partners(user_id: str,
                                      limit: int = Query(10, ge=1, le=50),
                                      rerank: bool = Query(False),
                                      types: str | None = Query(
                                          None,
                                          description="Comma-separated target types. Default: all "
                                                      "partner types (investor,corporation,"
                                                      "university,research_institution).")):
    return await _rag_match_by_user(user_id, PARTNER_TYPES, types, limit, rerank)


@app.get("/matches/investors/{user_id}/founders")
async def matches_investor_to_startups(user_id: str,
                                       limit: int = Query(10, ge=1, le=50),
                                       rerank: bool = Query(False),
                                       types: str | None = Query(
                                           None, description="Comma-separated target types. "
                                                             "Default: 'startup'.")):
    return await _rag_match_by_user(user_id, ["startup"], types, limit, rerank)

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Full VietNexus stack: postgres (pgvector) + gateway + matching-engine +
# extract agent + Python matching API (ai-data-platform) + frontend (nginx).
# Credentials default to the deal-flow-matchmaker ones (dealflow/dealflow).
# Host port 5433 by default — the old deal-flow-matchmaker-postgres-1 container
# still holds 5432 on this machine.
services:
  postgres:
    image: pgvector/pgvector:pg16
    container_name: dqplus-postgres
    user: postgres
    environment:
      POSTGRES_DB: ${DB_NAME:-dealflow}
      POSTGRES_USER: ${DB_USER:-dealflow}
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:[REDACTED]}
    ports:
      - "${DB_PORT:-5433}:5432"
    volumes:
      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data
      # ai-data-platform tables for the matching API — applied only on a fresh volume
      - ./ai-data-platform/migrations:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d:ro
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U ${DB_USER:-dealflow} -d ${DB_NAME:-dealflow}"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 10

  gateway:
    build: ./backend/gateway
    container_name: dqplus-gateway
    environment:
      PORT: 3000
      HOST: 0.0.0.0
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_PORT: 5432
      DB_NAME: ${DB_NAME:-dealflow}
      DB_USER: ${DB_USER:-dealflow}
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:[REDACTED]}
      DB_SSL: "false"
      JWT_SECRET: ${JWT_SECRET:[REDACTED]}
      JWT_EXPIRES_IN: 1d
      EXTRACT_SERVICE_URL: http://extract:3003
      MATCHING_API_URL: http://ai-data-platform:8000
    ports:
      - "${GATEWAY_PORT:-3000}:3000"
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "python3 -c \"import urllib.request; urllib.request.urlopen('http://localhost:3000/health')\" || exit 1"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  matching-engine:
    build: ./backend/matching-engine
    container_name: dqplus-matching-engine
    environment:
      PORT: 3002
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_PORT: 5432
      DB_NAME: ${DB_NAME:-dealflow}
      DB_USER: ${DB_USER:-dealflow}
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:[REDACTED]}
      DB_SSL: "false"
      MATCH_VECTOR_WEIGHT: ${MATCH_VECTOR_WEIGHT:-0.7}
      MATCH_ATTR_WEIGHT: ${MATCH_ATTR_WEIGHT:-0.3}
      MATCH_CANDIDATE_POOL: ${MATCH_CANDIDATE_POOL:-50}
      MATCHING_API_URL: http://ai-data-platform:8000
    ports:
      - "${MATCHING_PORT:-3002}:3002"
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "python3 -c \"import urllib.request; urllib.request.urlopen('http://localhost:3002/health')\" || exit 1"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  extract:
    build: ./backend/agent/extract
    container_name: dqplus-extract
    environment:
      PORT: 3003
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_PORT: 5432
      DB_NAME: ${DB_NAME:-dealflow}
      DB_USER: ${DB_USER:-dealflow}
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:[REDACTED]}
      DB_SSL: "false"
      # Empty key → deterministic feature-hash embedding fallback (no semantic
      # embeddings, but the profile → match flow still works).
      OPENAI_API_KEY: ${OPENAI_API_KEY:-}
      OPENAI_BASE_URL: ${OPENAI_BASE_URL:-https://mkp-api.fptcloud.com}
      OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL: ${OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL:-gpt-4o-mini}
      OPENAI_EMBEDDING_MODEL: ${OPENAI_EMBEDDING_MODEL:-text-embedding-3-small}
      EMBEDDING_DIM: ${EMBEDDING_DIM:-1536}
    ports:
      - "${EXTRACT_PORT:-3003}:3003"
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "python3 -c \"import urllib.request; urllib.request.urlopen('http://localhost:3003/health')\" || exit 1"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  ai-data-platform:
    build:
      context: ./ai-data-platform
      dockerfile: api/Dockerfile
    container_name: dqplus-ai-data-platform
    # FPT key + embedder/chat model scoped to this service only (multilingual-e5-large,
    # 1024-dim) — NOT via root .env, so `extract` (which needs a 1536 column) is untouched.
    # DATABASE_URL below overrides the .env's local value (compose environment wins).
    env_file:
      - ./ai-data-platform/.env
    environment:
      DATABASE_URL: postgres://${DB_USER:[REDACTED:db-password]@postgres:5432/${DB_NAME:-dealflow}
      # Same secret as the gateway: the dashboard (GET /) is admin-only and
      # verifies gateway-issued JWTs (role=admin, DB-provisioned).
      JWT_SECRET: ${JWT_SECRET:[REDACTED]}
    ports:
      - "${MATCHING_API_PORT:-8000}:8000"
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy

  # web is internal-only and scalable (no container_name / no published port) so
  # multiple replicas can run side by side during a rolling deploy. The `proxy`
  # service owns the public port and load-balances across replicas.
  web:
    build: ./frontend
    expose:
      - "80"
    depends_on:
      - gateway
      - matching-engine
      - extract
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "wget -q -O /dev/null http://127.0.0.1:80/ || exit 1"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 5s

  proxy:
    image: nginx:1.27-alpine
    container_name: dqplus-proxy
    volumes:
      - ./deploy/proxy.conf:/etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf:ro
    ports:
      - "${WEB_PORT:-5173}:80"
    depends_on:
      - web

volumes:
  pgdata:

# Full VietNexus stack: postgres (pgvector) + gateway + matching-engine +
# extract agent + Python matching API (ai-data-platform) + frontend (nginx).
# Credentials default to the deal-flow-matchmaker ones (dealflow/dealflow).
# Host port 5433 by default — the old deal-flow-matchmaker-postgres-1 container
# still holds 5432 on this machine.
services:
  postgres:
    image: pgvector/pgvector:pg16
    container_name: dqplus-postgres
    user: postgres
    environment:
      POSTGRES_DB: ${DB_NAME:-dealflow}
      POSTGRES_USER: ${DB_USER:-dealflow}
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:[REDACTED]}
    ports:
      - "${DB_PORT:-5433}:5432"
    volumes:
      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data
      # ai-data-platform tables for the matching API — applied only on a fresh volume
      - ./ai-data-platform/migrations:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d:ro
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U ${DB_USER:-dealflow} -d ${DB_NAME:-dealflow}"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 10

  gateway:
    build: ./backend/gateway
    container_name: dqplus-gateway
    environment:
      PORT: 3000
      HOST: 0.0.0.0
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_PORT: 5432
      DB_NAME: ${DB_NAME:-dealflow}
      DB_USER: ${DB_USER:-dealflow}
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:[REDACTED]}
      DB_SSL: "false"
      JWT_SECRET: ${JWT_SECRET:[REDACTED]}
      JWT_EXPIRES_IN: 1d
      EXTRACT_SERVICE_URL: http://extract:3003
      MATCHING_API_URL: http://ai-data-platform:8000
    ports:
      - "${GATEWAY_PORT:-3000}:3000"
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "python3 -c \"import urllib.request; urllib.request.urlopen('http://localhost:3000/health')\" || exit 1"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  # DISABLED — retired in favour of the ai-data-platform, which now serves /matches
  # (RAG/GraphRAG) directly. The `disabled` profile excludes it from the default
  # `docker compose up`; start it only with `--profile disabled` if ever needed.
  matching-engine:
    profiles: ["disabled"]
    build: ./backend/matching-engine
    container_name: dqplus-matching-engine
    environment:
      PORT: 3002
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_PORT: 5432
      DB_NAME: ${DB_NAME:-dealflow}
      DB_USER: ${DB_USER:-dealflow}
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:[REDACTED]}
      DB_SSL: "false"
      MATCH_VECTOR_WEIGHT: ${MATCH_VECTOR_WEIGHT:-0.7}
      MATCH_ATTR_WEIGHT: ${MATCH_ATTR_WEIGHT:-0.3}
      MATCH_CANDIDATE_POOL: ${MATCH_CANDIDATE_POOL:-50}
      MATCHING_API_URL: http://ai-data-platform:8000
    ports:
      - "${MATCHING_PORT:-3002}:3002"
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "python3 -c \"import urllib.request; urllib.request.urlopen('http://localhost:3002/health')\" || exit 1"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  extract:
    build: ./backend/agent/extract
    container_name: dqplus-extract
    environment:
      PORT: 3003
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_PORT: 5432
      DB_NAME: ${DB_NAME:-dealflow}
      DB_USER: ${DB_USER:-dealflow}
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:[REDACTED]}
      DB_SSL: "false"
      # Empty key → deterministic feature-hash embedding fallback (no semantic
      # embeddings, but the profile → match flow still works).
      OPENAI_API_KEY: ${OPENAI_API_KEY:-}
      OPENAI_BASE_URL: ${OPENAI_BASE_URL:-https://mkp-api.fptcloud.com}
      OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL: ${OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL:-gpt-4o-mini}
      OPENAI_EMBEDDING_MODEL: ${OPENAI_EMBEDDING_MODEL:-text-embedding-3-small}
      EMBEDDING_DIM: ${EMBEDDING_DIM:-1536}
    ports:
      - "${EXTRACT_PORT:-3003}:3003"
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "python3 -c \"import urllib.request; urllib.request.urlopen('http://localhost:3003/health')\" || exit 1"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  ai-data-platform:
    build:
      context: ./ai-data-platform
      dockerfile: api/Dockerfile
    container_name: dqplus-ai-data-platform
    # FPT key + embedder/chat model scoped to this service only (multilingual-e5-large,
    # 1024-dim) — NOT via root .env, so `extract` (which needs a 1536 column) is untouched.
    # DATABASE_URL below overrides the .env's local value (compose environment wins).
    env_file:
      - ./ai-data-platform/.env
    environment:
      DATABASE_URL: postgres://${DB_USER:[REDACTED:db-password]@postgres:5432/${DB_NAME:-dealflow}
      # Same secret as the gateway: the dashboard (GET /) is admin-only and
      # verifies gateway-issued JWTs (role=admin, DB-provisioned).
      JWT_SECRET: ${JWT_SECRET:[REDACTED]}
    ports:
      - "${MATCHING_API_PORT:-8000}:8000"
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy

  # web is internal-only and scalable (no container_name / no published port) so
  # multiple replicas can run side by side during a rolling deploy. The `proxy`
  # service owns the public port and load-balances across replicas.
  web:
    build: ./frontend
    expose:
      - "80"
    depends_on:
      - gateway
      - matching-engine
      - extract
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "wget -q -O /dev/null http://127.0.0.1:80/ || exit 1"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 5s

  proxy:
    image: nginx:1.27-alpine
    container_name: dqplus-proxy
    volumes:
      - ./deploy/proxy.conf:/etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf:ro
    ports:
      - "${WEB_PORT:-5173}:80"
    depends_on:
      - web

volumes:
  pgdata:

Now stop the old engine and handle the web deploy. Let me check the rolling-deploy script first (nginx.conf is baked into the web image):

input.command:
cd /home/delus/Documents/code/hackathon/dqplus-hackathon-2026

input.description: Read rolling-deploy script

content.content:
=== rolling-deploy.sh (does it build web?) ===
     1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2	# Zero-downtime rolling deploy of the `web` service behind the `proxy`.
     3	#
     4	# Strategy: build the new image, start an extra `web` replica alongside the
     5	# running one(s) and wait for it to become healthy, then gracefully drain the
     6	# OLD replica(s). The proxy load-balances across replicas via Docker DNS and
     7	# retries a sibling if one is draining, so the public port always has a healthy
     8	# backend — no request is dropped.
     9	#
    10	# We drain the old containers by ID rather than `--scale` down, because
    11	# `compose --scale N` removes the HIGHEST-numbered (newest) replicas, which
    12	# would throw away the container we just deployed.
    13	set -euo pipefail
    14	cd "$(dirname "$0")/.."
    15	
    16	SVC=web
    17	
    18	echo "==> Building new '$SVC' image"
    19	docker compose build "$SVC"
    20	
    21	echo "==> Ensuring proxy is running"
    22	docker compose up -d --no-deps proxy
    23	
    24	OLD_IDS="$(docker compose ps -q "$SVC" || true)"
    25	
    26	if [ -z "$OLD_IDS" ]; then
    27	  echo "==> No running '$SVC' replica — starting fresh"
    28	  docker compose up -d --no-deps --wait "$SVC"
    29	  docker compose ps "$SVC"
    30	  exit 0
    31	fi
    32	
    33	OLD_COUNT="$(printf '%s\n' "$OLD_IDS" | wc -l | tr -d ' ')"
    34	NEW_SCALE=$((OLD_COUNT + 1))
    35	
    36	echo "==> Starting new replica (scale $SVC=$NEW_SCALE, keeping old) and waiting for health"
    37	docker compose up -d --no-deps --no-recreate --scale "$SVC=$NEW_SCALE" --wait "$SVC"
    38	
    39	echo "==> New replica healthy. Draining old replica(s):"
    40	printf '    %s\n' $OLD_IDS
    41	# Graceful stop: in-flight requests finish; the proxy retries the new replica
    42	# for anything that arrives during the swap.
    43	docker stop $OLD_IDS >/dev/null
    44	docker rm $OLD_IDS >/dev/null
    45	
    46	echo "==> Reconciling desired state to scale=$SVC=1 (survivor kept, not recreated)"
    47	docker compose up -d --no-deps --no-recreate --scale "$SVC=1" "$SVC"
    48	
    49	echo "==> Done. Current '$SVC' replicas:"
    50	docker compose ps "$SVC"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# ==============================================================================
# tunnels.sh — Chạy 2 reverse SSH tunnel (autossh) song song, log ra file riêng.
#
# CÁCH DÙNG:
#   chmod +x tunnels.sh
#   ./tunnels.sh start   # khởi động 2 tunnel
#   ./tunnels.sh stop    # dừng 2 tunnel
#   ./tunnels.sh status  # kiểm tra đang chạy hay không
# ==============================================================================

set -euo pipefail

REMOTE_USER="ubuntu"
REMOTE_HOST="13.250.9.139"
# Đường dẫn tuyệt đối — pem tương đối sẽ hỏng nếu chạy script từ thư mục khác.
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
PEM_KEY="${SCRIPT_DIR}/hackathonai.pem"
LOG_DIR="${SCRIPT_DIR}/logs"
PID_DIR="${SCRIPT_DIR}/pids"

mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR" "$PID_DIR"
chmod 400 "$PEM_KEY" 2>/dev/null || true

# Định nghĩa các tunnel: "port_remote:host_local:port_local_dich:ten_goi_nho"
# PHẢI trỏ 127.0.0.1, KHÔNG dùng 192.168.0.79: WSL2 mirrored networking không
# route được LAN IP tới port do docker publish → VPS nginx 502 → web chết.
TUNNELS=(
  "5000:127.0.0.1:3000:web_backend_end"    # gateway
  "5001:127.0.0.1:8000:ssh_tunnel"         # ai-data-platform (uvicorn)
  "5002:127.0.0.1:3003:extract_agent"      # extract agent
  "5003:127.0.0.1:3002:matching_engine"    # matching engine
  "8443:127.0.0.1:5173:web_front_end"      # frontend (nginx)
)

start() {
  for t in "${TUNNELS[@]}"; do
    IFS=':' read -r rport lhost lport name <<< "$t"
    pidfile="${PID_DIR}/${name}.pid"

    if [[ -f "$pidfile" ]] && kill -0 "$(cat "$pidfile")" 2>/dev/null; then
      echo "[skip] $name đang chạy (PID $(cat "$pidfile"))"
      continue
    fi

    echo "[start] $name -> -R ${rport}:${lhost}:${lport}"
    nohup autossh -M 0 -N -R "${rport}:${lhost}:${lport}" \
      "${REMOTE_USER}@${REMOTE_HOST}" -i "$PEM_KEY" \
      -o "ServerAliveInterval 30" -o "ServerAliveCountMax 3" \
      -o "ExitOnForwardFailure yes" \
      > "${LOG_DIR}/${name}.log" 2>&1 &

    echo $! > "$pidfile"
    disown
    # VPS sshd khoá tạm (~1-2 phút) nếu kết nối dồn dập — giãn cách giữa các tunnel.
    sleep 5
  done
  status
}

stop() {
  for t in "${TUNNELS[@]}"; do
    IFS=':' read -r rport lhost lport name <<< "$t"
    pidfile="${PID_DIR}/${name}.pid"
    if [[ -f "$pidfile" ]]; then
      pid="$(cat "$pidfile")"
      if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "[stop] $name (PID $pid)"
        kill "$pid" || true
      fi
      rm -f "$pidfile"
    else
      echo "[skip] $name không có pidfile, có thể chưa chạy"
    fi
  done
  # Dọn luôn các tunnel autossh tới VPS này được chạy tay (không có pidfile),
  # tránh chiếm port remote khiến start mới bị ExitOnForwardFailure.
  pkill -f "autossh.*${REMOTE_HOST}" 2>/dev/null || true
}

status() {
  for t in "${TUNNELS[@]}"; do
    IFS=':' read -r rport lhost lport name <<< "$t"
    pidfile="${PID_DIR}/${name}.pid"
    if [[ -f "$pidfile" ]] && kill -0 "$(cat "$pidfile")" 2>/dev/null; then
      echo "[up]   $name (PID $(cat "$pidfile")) -> -R ${rport}:${lhost}:${lport}"
    else
      echo "[down] $name"
    fi
  done
}

case "${1:-start}" in
  start)  start ;;
  stop)   stop ;;
  status) status ;;
  *) echo "Dùng: $0 {start|stop|status}"; exit 1 ;;
esac

input.new_string:
Until embedded, the `/matches` RAG endpoints return empty and
`store.py`'s best-effort embed-on-upsert logs `embed_on_upsert_failed` (harmless).

**Cutover done (2026-07-19): matching-engine RETIRED, platform serves /matches.**
- `matching-engine` is disabled in docker-compose (`profiles: ["disabled"]`), container removed.
- Platform now has GET `/matches/founders/{user_id}/investors` + `/matches/investors/{user_id}/founders`
  that SELF-FETCH the requester's profile from `extracted_profiles` by gateway user UUID
  (`embedding_text` + `attributes`), embed with e5-1024, RAG over the entity corpus. Founders
  route defaults to ALL partner types (investor/corporation/university/research_institution).
  Still 404s when no extracted_profiles row, preserving the app's extract-then-retry flow.
  (POST body-based variants also still exist.)
- **Public routing gotcha**: the VPS nginx (13.250.9.139) routes each `/api/*` prefix to a
  DEDICATED reverse-SSH tunnel, NOT through the web container's nginx. `/api/matches` → VPS
  port 5003. So `frontend/nginx.conf` (baked into `web`) does NOT govern the public /matches
  path. The fix was to repoint the `5003` tunnel in `deploy/port-forward.sh` from `:3002`
  (dead matching-engine) to `:8000` (platform). Editing `frontend/nginx.conf` only affects
  the local `:5173` proxy chain, not the public URL.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
---
name: adp-rag-graphrag
description: ai-data-platform now has pgvector RAG + GraphRAG + dashboard graph view; activation needs migration 004 + backfill
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 7c5aee8d-22dd-403c-b0b2-a862987e863f
  modified: 2026-07-18T19:29:35.371Z
---

`ai-data-platform` gained three capabilities (built 2026-07-19), ported/extended from
the fork `NghiemNgocDuc/vietname-ai-innovation`:

- **RAG**: `spine/embedding.py` (FPT `multilingual-e5-large` **1024-dim** — NOT
  text-embedding-3-small, which FPT does not host; deterministic blake2b feature-hash
  fallback when no `OPENAI_API_KEY`), `spine/matcher/embedding_matcher.py`
  (pgvector KNN over `entities` + rule attribute score, optional LLM rerank), and
  `POST /matches/founders/{id}/investors` + `/matches/investors/{id}/founders` on the
  API (caller passes the profile in the request body; returns the backend matching-engine
  shape score/vectorScore/attributeScore/reasons + id/name/type/rationale_*).
- **GraphRAG**: `spine/graph.py` (BFS over `edges` with name-slug re-resolution) +
  `GET /entities/{id}/graph-matches`.
- **Semantic /discover** + dashboard graph card & UI (fit slider, toggleable sidebar).

**Activation is NOT automatic**: `entities.embedding` starts NULL. Must (1) apply
`migrations/004_embeddings.sql` (auto-applied by `bootstrap.py` glob on fresh boot; a
running DB needs it applied), then (2) run `uv run python scripts/embed_entities.py`
(source `ai-data-platform/.env` first — spine reads `os.environ`, no dotenv autoload).
Done once on 2026-07-19: all 115 seed entities embedded in `dqplus-postgres` (:5433).

**FPT gotchas (learned the hard way):**
- FPT (`mkp-api.fptcloud.com`) sits behind **Cloudflare** → the default Python-urllib
  User-Agent gets a 403 "error code: 1010". Every FPT call MUST send a browser UA
  (done in embedding.py / embedding_matcher.py / semantic.py).
- FPT hosts embedders `multilingual-e5-large` and `Vietnamese_Embedding` (both 1024-dim),
  NOT OpenAI models. Chat models: `gemma-4-31B-it`, `Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct`, `GLM-5.2`,
  `DeepSeek-V4-Flash`, `gpt-oss-120b`, etc. (`GET /models` to list). Column is `vector(1024)`.
- Corpus and query MUST use the same embedder. So the deployed `matching-api` container
  needs the SAME `OPENAI_API_KEY`/model as the backfill, or query vectors won't match the
  stored corpus. As of 2026-07-19 the running container has OLD code + no key → rebuild
  (`docker compose up -d --build matching-api`) + pass the key before the API serves matches.

Until embedded, the `/matches` RAG endpoints return empty and
`store.py`'s best-effort embed-on-upsert logs `embed_on_upsert_failed` (harmless).

Related: [[vietnexus-project]].

content.text:
## Done — matching-engine retired, ai-data-platform serves `/matches` live

Your exact public URL now returns the new RAG behavior (verified above). Here's the full cutover:

**Code (platform now self-serves the public contract):**
- `api/app.py` — added **GET** `/matches/founders/{user_id}/investors` + `/matches/investors/{user_id}/founders`. They self-fetch the requester's profile from `extracted_profiles` by gateway UUID (no body needed), embed with e5-1024, and RAG over the corpus. Founders → **all partner types**. Still 404s when there's no profile, preserving the app's extract-then-retry flow.
- `embedding_matcher.match()` — accepts a pre-fetched `query_text`/`query_norm` for the by-UUID path.

**Config:**
- `docker-compose.yml` — `matching-engine` disabled via `profiles: ["disabled"]`, container removed; `web` now depends on `ai-data-platform`.
- `frontend/nginx.conf` — `/api/matches` → `ai-data-platform:8000` (governs the local `:5173` proxy chain).

**The routing gotcha that made your public test still fail after the container work:**
The VPS nginx routes each `/api/*` prefix to a **dedicated reverse-SSH tunnel**, *not* through the web container's nginx. `/api/matches` → VPS port **5003** → the (now-dead) matching-engine, so it timed out. The fix was repointing the `5003` tunnel in `deploy/port-forward.sh` from `:3002` → `:8000`. Editing the web nginx alone would never have fixed the public URL.

**Verified:** local `:5173` proxy chain ✓, public `https://dqplus.ddns.net/api/matches/...` ✓.

### Two things to flag
1. **Matches are now entity slugs** (`investor:fpt-ventures`, name, type) rather than gateway user IDs. The response keeps `score/vectorScore/attributeScore/reasons`, but if the frontend match cards link off `userId` to open a user profile, they'll need a small tweak to render/link entities.
2. **`port-forward.sh` is local/gitignored** — this tunnel repoint lives only on this machine. The cleaner long-term fix is to update the VPS nginx to point `/api/matches` → the platform's own `5001` tunnel and drop the redundant `5003`. (Right now `5001` and `5003` both forward to `:8000` — harmless.)

The code/config edits (`docker-compose.yml`, `frontend/nginx.conf`, `api/app.py`, `embedding_matcher.py`) are **uncommitted** in the working tree. Want me to commit them? And shall I take a quick look at the frontend match-card rendering to see whether it needs the entity-vs-user tweak?

content.text:
Here's the current reality, which changes what "correct" means: there's **only one database** right now. A single `dqplus-postgres` (host `:5433`) holds a single `dealflow` DB with **both** backend user info (`users`=23, `profiles`=19, `extracted_profiles`=7) **and** platform data (`entities`, `edges`, `matches`, `sagas`, `jobs`). The `:5432` on your host is a *different, unrelated* old project's container (`deal-flow-matchmaker-postgres-1`), not part of this stack.

So "5432 → platform, 5433 → backend/user info" means **splitting one DB into two separate Postgres instances** — which is a real data migration, and it directly breaks the cutover I just shipped: the platform's `GET /matches/founders/{uuid}/investors` reads `extracted_profiles` **by SQL from the same DB** as `entities`. If user info moves to a separate backend DB, the platform can no longer do that.

That's a genuine fork, so I need your call before touching it:

questions.question: Splitting into two Postgres (platform :5432, backend/user-info :5433) means the platform can't read extracted_profiles by SQL for the /matches endpoint anymore. How should this work?

options.label: Split; platform dual-connects
options.description: Two Postgres: backend DB (users/profiles/extracted_profiles) on :5432... wait — you said platform=:5432. Backend/user DB on :5433, platform DB (entities/matches/sagas) on :5432. Migrate the current dealflow data into the two. Platform opens a SECOND read-only connection to the backend DB to fetch a user's extracted_profile for matches. Least refactoring; keeps the /matches endpoint working.

options.label: Split; platform fetches via HTTP
options.description: Same two-DB split + data migration, but the platform gets the user's profile over HTTP from the extract service (GET /extracted/:userId) instead of a DB connection. Cleaner decoupling (matches the original 'platform gets profile from backend' idea), but adds a network hop and depends on extract being up.

options.label: Keep one shared DB
options.description: Don't split. Everything stays in the single dealflow DB. If you only want the host port to read 5432 instead of 5433, I'll just change the published port mapping (note: :5432 is currently held by the unrelated old container, so it must be freed or a different port used). No migration, nothing breaks.

questions.question: The two DBs would need the current data divided. How should existing data be handled?

options.label: Migrate existing data
options.description: Copy users/profiles/extracted_profiles into the new backend DB and entities/edges/matches/sagas/jobs into the platform DB, then verify counts before dropping the originals. Preserves the 23 users, 7 extracted profiles, 87 entities, and their embeddings.

options.label: Fresh empty DBs + re-seed
options.description: Start both DBs empty; re-run bootstrap/seed for the platform and re-register/re-extract users. Simpler compose, but loses current data (including the FPT embeddings I just backfilled) unless re-generated.

options.label: N/A — keeping one DB
options.description: Choose this if you picked 'Keep one shared DB' above — no migration needed.

content.text: Everything's classified and verified. This is a careful live migration — I'll do it step by step. Starting with the compose topology: keep `postgres` as the backend DB (:5433, drop its platform-migration init mount), add a new `platform-postgres` (:5432), and repoint the platform at it + tell it where `extract` lives.

      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:[REDACTED]}
    ports:
      - "${DB_PORT:-5433}:5432"

# Backend DB — user info only (users / profiles / extracted_profiles), used by

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Full VietNexus stack: postgres (pgvector) + gateway + matching-engine +
# extract agent + Python matching API (ai-data-platform) + frontend (nginx).
# Credentials default to the deal-flow-matchmaker ones (dealflow/dealflow).
# Host port 5433 by default — the old deal-flow-matchmaker-postgres-1 container
# still holds 5432 on this machine.
services:
  postgres:
    image: pgvector/pgvector:pg16
    container_name: dqplus-postgres
    user: postgres
    environment:
      POSTGRES_DB: ${DB_NAME:-dealflow}
      POSTGRES_USER: ${DB_USER:-dealflow}
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:[REDACTED]}
    ports:
      - "${DB_PORT:-5433}:5432"
    volumes:
      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data
      # ai-data-platform tables for the matching API — applied only on a fresh volume
      - ./ai-data-platform/migrations:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d:ro
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U ${DB_USER:-dealflow} -d ${DB_NAME:-dealflow}"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 10

  gateway:
    build: ./backend/gateway
    container_name: dqplus-gateway
    environment:
      PORT: 3000
      HOST: 0.0.0.0
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_PORT: 5432
      DB_NAME: ${DB_NAME:-dealflow}
      DB_USER: ${DB_USER:-dealflow}
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:[REDACTED]}
      DB_SSL: "false"
      JWT_SECRET: ${JWT_SECRET:[REDACTED]}
      JWT_EXPIRES_IN: 1d
      EXTRACT_SERVICE_URL: http://extract:3003
      MATCHING_API_URL: http://ai-data-platform:8000
    ports:
      - "${GATEWAY_PORT:-3000}:3000"
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "python3 -c \"import urllib.request; urllib.request.urlopen('http://localhost:3000/health')\" || exit 1"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  # DISABLED — retired in favour of the ai-data-platform, which now serves /matches
  # (RAG/GraphRAG) directly. The `disabled` profile excludes it from the default
  # `docker compose up`; start it only with `--profile disabled` if ever needed.
  matching-engine:
    profiles: ["disabled"]
    build: ./backend/matching-engine
    container_name: dqplus-matching-engine
    environment:
      PORT: 3002
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_PORT: 5432
      DB_NAME: ${DB_NAME:-dealflow}
      DB_USER: ${DB_USER:-dealflow}
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:[REDACTED]}
      DB_SSL: "false"
      MATCH_VECTOR_WEIGHT: ${MATCH_VECTOR_WEIGHT:-0.7}
      MATCH_ATTR_WEIGHT: ${MATCH_ATTR_WEIGHT:-0.3}
      MATCH_CANDIDATE_POOL: ${MATCH_CANDIDATE_POOL:-50}
      MATCHING_API_URL: http://ai-data-platform:8000
    ports:
      - "${MATCHING_PORT:-3002}:3002"
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "python3 -c \"import urllib.request; urllib.request.urlopen('http://localhost:3002/health')\" || exit 1"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  extract:
    build: ./backend/agent/extract
    container_name: dqplus-extract
    environment:
      PORT: 3003
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_PORT: 5432
      DB_NAME: ${DB_NAME:-dealflow}
      DB_USER: ${DB_USER:-dealflow}
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:[REDACTED]}
      DB_SSL: "false"
      # Empty key → deterministic feature-hash embedding fallback (no semantic
      # embeddings, but the profile → match flow still works).
      OPENAI_API_KEY: ${OPENAI_API_KEY:-}
      OPENAI_BASE_URL: ${OPENAI_BASE_URL:-https://mkp-api.fptcloud.com}
      OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL: ${OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL:-gpt-4o-mini}
      OPENAI_EMBEDDING_MODEL: ${OPENAI_EMBEDDING_MODEL:-text-embedding-3-small}
      EMBEDDING_DIM: ${EMBEDDING_DIM:-1536}
    ports:
      - "${EXTRACT_PORT:-3003}:3003"
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "python3 -c \"import urllib.request; urllib.request.urlopen('http://localhost:3003/health')\" || exit 1"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  ai-data-platform:
    build:
      context: ./ai-data-platform
      dockerfile: api/Dockerfile
    container_name: dqplus-ai-data-platform
    # FPT key + embedder/chat model scoped to this service only (multilingual-e5-large,
    # 1024-dim) — NOT via root .env, so `extract` (which needs a 1536 column) is untouched.
    # DATABASE_URL below overrides the .env's local value (compose environment wins).
    env_file:
      - ./ai-data-platform/.env
    environment:
      DATABASE_URL: postgres://${DB_USER:[REDACTED:db-password]@postgres:5432/${DB_NAME:-dealflow}
      # Same secret as the gateway: the dashboard (GET /) is admin-only and
      # verifies gateway-issued JWTs (role=admin, DB-provisioned).
      JWT_SECRET: ${JWT_SECRET:[REDACTED]}
    ports:
      - "${MATCHING_API_PORT:-8000}:8000"
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy

  # web is internal-only and scalable (no container_name / no published port) so
  # multiple replicas can run side by side during a rolling deploy. The `proxy`
  # service owns the public port and load-balances across replicas.
  web:
    build: ./frontend
    expose:
      - "80"
    depends_on:
      - gateway
      - ai-data-platform
      - extract
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "wget -q -O /dev/null http://127.0.0.1:80/ || exit 1"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 5s

  proxy:
    image: nginx:1.27-alpine
    container_name: dqplus-proxy
    volumes:
      - ./deploy/proxy.conf:/etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf:ro
    ports:
      - "${WEB_PORT:-5173}:80"
    depends_on:
      - web

volumes:
  pgdata:

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Full VietNexus stack: postgres (pgvector) + gateway + matching-engine +
# extract agent + Python matching API (ai-data-platform) + frontend (nginx).
# Credentials default to the deal-flow-matchmaker ones (dealflow/dealflow).
# Host port 5433 by default — the old deal-flow-matchmaker-postgres-1 container
# still holds 5432 on this machine.
services:
  # Backend DB — user info only (users / profiles / extracted_profiles), used by
  # gateway + extract. Host port 5433. (gateway/extract self-create their tables.)
  postgres:
    image: pgvector/pgvector:pg16
    container_name: dqplus-postgres
    user: postgres
    environment:
      POSTGRES_DB: ${DB_NAME:-dealflow}
      POSTGRES_USER: ${DB_USER:-dealflow}
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:[REDACTED]}
    ports:
      - "${DB_PORT:-5433}:5432"
    volumes:
      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U ${DB_USER:-dealflow} -d ${DB_NAME:-dealflow}"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 10

  # Platform DB — ai-data-platform data (entities / edges / matches / sagas / jobs …),
  # used by ai-data-platform only. Host port 5432. Separate from the backend user DB;
  # the platform reads user profiles from the extract service over HTTP, not by SQL.
  platform-postgres:
    image: pgvector/pgvector:pg16
    container_name: dqplus-platform-postgres
    user: postgres
    environment:
      POSTGRES_DB: ${DB_NAME:-dealflow}
      POSTGRES_USER: ${DB_USER:-dealflow}
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:[REDACTED]}
    ports:
      - "${PLATFORM_DB_PORT:-5432}:5432"
    volumes:
      - platform_pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U ${DB_USER:-dealflow} -d ${DB_NAME:-dealflow}"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 10

  gateway:
    build: ./backend/gateway
    container_name: dqplus-gateway
    environment:
      PORT: 3000
      HOST: 0.0.0.0
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_PORT: 5432
      DB_NAME: ${DB_NAME:-dealflow}
      DB_USER: ${DB_USER:-dealflow}
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:[REDACTED]}
      DB_SSL: "false"
      JWT_SECRET: ${JWT_SECRET:[REDACTED]}
      JWT_EXPIRES_IN: 1d
      EXTRACT_SERVICE_URL: http://extract:3003
      MATCHING_API_URL: http://ai-data-platform:8000
    ports:
      - "${GATEWAY_PORT:-3000}:3000"
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "python3 -c \"import urllib.request; urllib.request.urlopen('http://localhost:3000/health')\" || exit 1"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  # DISABLED — retired in favour of the ai-data-platform, which now serves /matches
  # (RAG/GraphRAG) directly. The `disabled` profile excludes it from the default
  # `docker compose up`; start it only with `--profile disabled` if ever needed.
  matching-engine:
    profiles: ["disabled"]
    build: ./backend/matching-engine
    container_name: dqplus-matching-engine
    environment:
      PORT: 3002
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_PORT: 5432
      DB_NAME: ${DB_NAME:-dealflow}
      DB_USER: ${DB_USER:-dealflow}
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:[REDACTED]}
      DB_SSL: "false"
      MATCH_VECTOR_WEIGHT: ${MATCH_VECTOR_WEIGHT:-0.7}
      MATCH_ATTR_WEIGHT: ${MATCH_ATTR_WEIGHT:-0.3}
      MATCH_CANDIDATE_POOL: ${MATCH_CANDIDATE_POOL:-50}
      MATCHING_API_URL: http://ai-data-platform:8000
    ports:
      - "${MATCHING_PORT:-3002}:3002"
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "python3 -c \"import urllib.request; urllib.request.urlopen('http://localhost:3002/health')\" || exit 1"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  extract:
    build: ./backend/agent/extract
    container_name: dqplus-extract
    environment:
      PORT: 3003
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_PORT: 5432
      DB_NAME: ${DB_NAME:-dealflow}
      DB_USER: ${DB_USER:-dealflow}
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:[REDACTED]}
      DB_SSL: "false"
      # Empty key → deterministic feature-hash embedding fallback (no semantic
      # embeddings, but the profile → match flow still works).
      OPENAI_API_KEY: ${OPENAI_API_KEY:-}
      OPENAI_BASE_URL: ${OPENAI_BASE_URL:-https://mkp-api.fptcloud.com}
      OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL: ${OPENAI_CHAT_MODEL:-gpt-4o-mini}
      OPENAI_EMBEDDING_MODEL: ${OPENAI_EMBEDDING_MODEL:-text-embedding-3-small}
      EMBEDDING_DIM: ${EMBEDDING_DIM:-1536}
    ports:
      - "${EXTRACT_PORT:-3003}:3003"
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "python3 -c \"import urllib.request; urllib.request.urlopen('http://localhost:3003/health')\" || exit 1"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  ai-data-platform:
    build:
      context: ./ai-data-platform
      dockerfile: api/Dockerfile
    container_name: dqplus-ai-data-platform
    # FPT key + embedder/chat model scoped to this service only (multilingual-e5-large,
    # 1024-dim) — NOT via root .env, so `extract` (which needs a 1536 column) is untouched.
    # DATABASE_URL below overrides the .env's local value (compose environment wins).
    env_file:
      - ./ai-data-platform/.env
    environment:
      DATABASE_URL: postgres://${DB_USER:[REDACTED:db-password]@platform-postgres:5432/${DB_NAME:-dealflow}
      # User profiles live in the backend DB now; the platform fetches them over HTTP
      # from the extract service (GET /extracted/:userId) for the /matches endpoints.
      EXTRACT_SERVICE_URL: http://extract:3003
      # Same secret as the gateway: the dashboard (GET /) is admin-only and
      # verifies gateway-issued JWTs (role=admin, DB-provisioned).
      JWT_SECRET: ${JWT_SECRET:[REDACTED]}
    ports:
      - "${MATCHING_API_PORT:-8000}:8000"
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy

  # web is internal-only and scalable (no container_name / no published port) so
  # multiple replicas can run side by side during a rolling deploy. The `proxy`
  # service owns the public port and load-balances across replicas.
  web:
    build: ./frontend
    expose:
      - "80"
    depends_on:
      - gateway
      - ai-data-platform
      - extract
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "wget -q -O /dev/null http://127.0.0.1:80/ || exit 1"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 5s

  proxy:
    image: nginx:1.27-alpine
    container_name: dqplus-proxy
    volumes:
      - ./deploy/proxy.conf:/etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf:ro
    ports:
      - "${WEB_PORT:-5173}:80"
    depends_on:
      - web

volumes:
  pgdata:

async def _rag_match_by_user(user_id: str, default_types: list[str], types: str | None,
                             limit: int, rerank: bool) -> dict:
    row = await _pool(app).fetchrow(
        "SELECT role, attributes, embedding_text FROM extracted_profiles WHERE user_id = $1",
        user_id,
    )
    if row is None:
        # Same 404 as the old engine so the app's extract-then-retry flow still triggers.
        raise HTTPException(404, "No extracted profile for user; run extraction first")
    attrs = row["attributes"]
    if isinstance(attrs, str):
        attrs = json.loads(attrs or "{}")
    result = await embedding_matcher.match(
        _pool(app),
        query_text=row["embedding_text"] or "",
        query_norm=_norm_from_attrs(attrs or {}),
        target_types=_target_types(default_types, types),
        limit=limit,
        rerank=rerank,
    )
    return {"userId": user_id, "role": row["role"], "matches": result["matches"]}

content.text: Config valid, compiles. Now the migration. Free host `:5432` (stop the stale predecessor container), bring up `platform-postgres`, and prep the vector extension:

input.command:
cd /home/delus/Documents/code/hackathon/dqplus-hackathon-2026
echo "=== free host :5432 (stop stale predecessor container) ==="
docker stop deal-flow-matchmaker-postgres-1 2>&1 || echo "(not running)"
echo
echo "=== bring up platform-postgres ==="
docker compose up -d platform-postgres 2>&1 | tail -3
echo "--- wait for healthy ---"
for i in $(seq 1 20); do
  h=$(docker inspect -f '{{.State.Health.Status}}' dqplus-platform-postgres 2>/dev/null)
  echo "  health: $h"; [ "$h" = "healthy" ] && break; sleep 2
done
echo "=== create vector extension in platform DB ==="
docker exec dqplus-platform-postgres psql -U dealflow -d dealflow -c "CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector;" 2>&1
input.description: Free 5432, start platform-postgres, create vector ext

content.text: Found it — zsh doesn't word-split the unquoted `$TABLES` variable, so pg_dump saw one bogus `-t` pattern. Inlining the flags:

input.command:
cd /home/delus/Documents/code/hackathon/dqplus-hackathon-2026
echo "=== migrate: pg_dump (inlined -t flags) → restore into platform-postgres ==="
docker exec dqplus-postgres pg_dump -U dealflow -d dealflow --no-owner --no-privileges \
  -t public.entities -t public.edges -t public.artifacts -t public.matches -t public.matches_v2 \
  -t public.saga_instances -t public.jobs -t public.events -t public.llm_usage -t public.workers \
  -t public.apps -t public.schema_migrations 2>/tmp/dump.err \
  | docker exec -i dqplus-platform-postgres psql -U dealflow -d dealflow -v ON_ERROR_STOP=0 >/tmp/restore.log 2>&1
echo "dump stderr:"; cat /tmp/dump.err | head
echo "restore errors (excl. 'already exists'):"; grep -iE 'error|fatal' /tmp/restore.log | grep -viE 'already exists' | head
echo
echo "=== verify counts: platform-postgres vs backend ==="
for t in entities edges matches matches_v2 saga_instances jobs events llm_usage workers apps artifacts; do
  b=$(docker exec dqplus-postgres psql -U dealflow -d dealflow -tA -c "SELECT count(*) FROM $t")
  p=$(docker exec dqplus-platform-postgres psql -U dealflow -d dealflow -tA -c "SELECT count(*) FROM $t")
  flag=$([ "$b" = "$p" ] && echo OK || echo MISMATCH)
  printf "  %-16s backend=%-6s platform=%-6s %s\n" "$t" "$b" "$p" "$flag"
done
echo "--- entities embedding coverage in platform ---"
docker exec dqplus-platform-postgres psql -U dealflow -d dealflow -tA -c "SELECT 'embedded='||count(embedding)||'/'||count(*) FROM entities; SELECT 'hnsw='||EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM pg_indexes WHERE tablename='entities' AND indexname='idx_entities_embedding');"

input.old_string: **Cutover done (2026-07-19): matching-engine RETIRED, platform serves /matches.**
input.new_string:
**DB SPLIT (2026-07-19): two separate Postgres, no longer a shared DB.**
- `dqplus-postgres` (host **:5433**) = BACKEND DB — ONLY `users`/`profiles`/`extracted_profiles`
  (gateway + extract; they self-create their tables). DB_HOST=postgres internally.
- `dqplus-platform-postgres` (host **:5432**) = PLATFORM DB — `entities`/`edges`/`matches`/
  `matches_v2`/`saga_instances`/`jobs`/`events`/`artifacts`/`llm_usage`/`workers`/`apps`/
  `schema_migrations`. ai-data-platform DATABASE_URL → `platform-postgres:5432`.
- The platform NO LONGER reads `extracted_profiles` by SQL. It fetches user profiles from
  the extract service over HTTP: `GET {EXTRACT_SERVICE_URL}/extracted/:userId`
  (`EXTRACT_SERVICE_URL=http://extract:3003`), used by `_rag_match_by_user` in api/app.py.
- To free host :5432, the stale predecessor container `deal-flow-matchmaker-postgres-1`
  was stopped. (Supersedes the old CLAUDE.md gotcha that 5433 was used because 5432 was taken.)
- Data was migrated via `pg_dump` of the platform tables from the old shared DB into
  platform-postgres. NOTE: zsh does NOT word-split unquoted `$VAR`, so inline pg_dump `-t`
  flags (a `-t a -t b` variable becomes one bad pattern → "no matching tables were found").

**Cutover done (2026-07-19): matching-engine RETIRED, platform serves /matches.**
