toolUseResult.originalFile:
---
name: adp-rag-graphrag
description: ai-data-platform now has pgvector RAG + GraphRAG + dashboard graph view; activation needs migration 004 + backfill
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 7c5aee8d-22dd-403c-b0b2-a862987e863f
  modified: 2026-07-18T20:04:56.794Z
---

`ai-data-platform` gained three capabilities (built 2026-07-19), ported/extended from
the fork `NghiemNgocDuc/vietname-ai-innovation`:

- **RAG**: `spine/embedding.py` (FPT `multilingual-e5-large` **1024-dim** — NOT
  text-embedding-3-small, which FPT does not host; deterministic blake2b feature-hash
  fallback when no `OPENAI_API_KEY`), `spine/matcher/embedding_matcher.py`
  (pgvector KNN over `entities` + rule attribute score, optional LLM rerank), and
  `POST /matches/founders/{id}/investors` + `/matches/investors/{id}/founders` on the
  API (caller passes the profile in the request body; returns the backend matching-engine
  shape score/vectorScore/attributeScore/reasons + id/name/type/rationale_*).
- **GraphRAG**: `spine/graph.py` (BFS over `edges` with name-slug re-resolution) +
  `GET /entities/{id}/graph-matches`.
- **Semantic /discover** + dashboard graph card & UI (fit slider, toggleable sidebar).

**Activation is NOT automatic**: `entities.embedding` starts NULL. Must (1) apply
`migrations/004_embeddings.sql` (auto-applied by `bootstrap.py` glob on fresh boot; a
running DB needs it applied), then (2) run `uv run python scripts/embed_entities.py`
(source `ai-data-platform/.env` first — spine reads `os.environ`, no dotenv autoload).
Done once on 2026-07-19: all 115 seed entities embedded in `dqplus-postgres` (:5433).

**FPT gotchas (learned the hard way):**
- FPT (`mkp-api.fptcloud.com`) sits behind **Cloudflare** → the default Python-urllib
  User-Agent gets a 403 "error code: 1010". Every FPT call MUST send a browser UA
  (done in embedding.py / embedding_matcher.py / semantic.py).
- FPT hosts embedders `multilingual-e5-large` and `Vietnamese_Embedding` (both 1024-dim),
  NOT OpenAI models. Chat models: `gemma-4-31B-it`, `Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct`, `GLM-5.2`,
  `DeepSeek-V4-Flash`, `gpt-oss-120b`, etc. (`GET /models` to list). Column is `vector(1024)`.
- Corpus and query MUST use the same embedder. So the deployed `matching-api` container
  needs the SAME `OPENAI_API_KEY`/model as the backfill, or query vectors won't match the
  stored corpus. As of 2026-07-19 the running container has OLD code + no key → rebuild
  (`docker compose up -d --build matching-api`) + pass the key before the API serves matches.

Until embedded, the `/matches` RAG endpoints return empty and
`store.py`'s best-effort embed-on-upsert logs `embed_on_upsert_failed` (harmless).

**Cutover done (2026-07-19): matching-engine RETIRED, platform serves /matches.**
- `matching-engine` is disabled in docker-compose (`profiles: ["disabled"]`), container removed.
- Platform now has GET `/matches/founders/{user_id}/investors` + `/matches/investors/{user_id}/founders`
  that SELF-FETCH the requester's profile from `extracted_profiles` by gateway user UUID
  (`embedding_text` + `attributes`), embed with e5-1024, RAG over the entity corpus. Founders
  route defaults to ALL partner types (investor/corporation/university/research_institution).
  Still 404s when no extracted_profiles row, preserving the app's extract-then-retry flow.
  (POST body-based variants also still exist.)
- **Public routing gotcha**: the VPS nginx (13.250.9.139) routes each `/api/*` prefix to a
  DEDICATED reverse-SSH tunnel, NOT through the web container's nginx. `/api/matches` → VPS
  port 5003. So `frontend/nginx.conf` (baked into `web`) does NOT govern the public /matches
  path. The fix was to repoint the `5003` tunnel in `deploy/port-forward.sh` from `:3002`
  (dead matching-engine) to `:8000` (platform). Editing `frontend/nginx.conf` only affects
  the local `:5173` proxy chain, not the public URL.

Related: [[vietnexus-project]].

content.text:
Done — the Postgres split is complete, migrated, and live. Here's the final topology:

## Two databases now
| DB | Container (host port) | Tables | Used by |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Backend / user info** | `dqplus-postgres` (**:5433**) | `users` (23), `profiles`, `extracted_profiles` (7) | gateway, extract |
| **Platform** | `dqplus-platform-postgres` (**:5432**) | `entities`, `edges`, `matches`, `matches_v2`, `saga_instances`, `jobs`, `events`, `artifacts`, `llm_usage`, `workers`, `apps`, `schema_migrations` | ai-data-platform |

## What changed
- **compose**: added `platform-postgres` (:5432) + `platform_pgdata` volume; `postgres` is now backend-only (dropped its platform-migration init mount); platform repointed to `platform-postgres:5432` + `EXTRACT_SERVICE_URL=http://extract:3003`.
- **app.py**: `GET /matches/founders/{id}/investors` now fetches the profile from **extract over HTTP** (`GET /extracted/:userId`) instead of SQL — since user data lives in the backend DB. 404 still preserved for the extract-then-retry flow.
- **data**: all 11 platform tables migrated (counts verified identical, `entities` 59/59 embedded, HNSW index intact), then dropped from the backend DB.
- **freed :5432** by stopping the stale predecessor container `deal-flow-matchmaker-postgres-1`.

## Verified

# VietNexus — project rules for Claude

Innovation OS matching Vietnamese startups with investors/partners/universities.
Monorepo. Full stack runs in Docker (`docker-compose.yml`). Public site:
**https://dqplus.ddns.net**. Read [`docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`](docs/ARCHITECTURE.md)
before making structural changes.

## Deployment — read before touching the live site

The public chain is: **VPS nginx → autossh SSH tunnel → local `:5173` → `proxy`
container → `web` replicas**. Only one container can bind a host port, so the
`web` service is internal-only and scaled behind the `proxy`.

- **Deploy a frontend change with `bash deploy/rolling-deploy.sh`.** It rolls a
  new `web` replica in and drains the old one with zero dropped requests. **Never**
  run `docker compose up -d --build web` against the live site — it stops the old
  container before the new one binds (seconds of downtime).
- **`web` must stay scalable**: no `container_name`, no published `ports:` — keep
  `expose: "80"`. The `proxy` service owns the public port (`WEB_PORT`, default 5173).
- **Edit `deploy/proxy.conf` → apply with `docker exec dqplus-proxy nginx -s reload`**
  (zero downtime). Recreating the `proxy` container briefly drops `:5173`.
- **The SSH tunnels** are managed by `deploy/port-forward.sh {start|stop|status}`
  (targets `127.0.0.1`, not the LAN IP — WSL2 mirrored networking makes
  docker-published ports unreachable via the LAN IP). Don't start tunnels by hand.
  `deploy/hackathonai.pem` and `deploy/port-forward.sh` are local-only (gitignored).

## Gotchas learned the hard way

- **Container healthchecks must hit `127.0.0.1`, not `localhost`.** `localhost`
  resolves to `::1`; the services bind IPv4 only, so `localhost` reports a healthy
  container as unhealthy and hangs `docker compose --wait`.
- **`docker compose --scale web=N` scales *down* by removing the newest replica.**
  To roll, drain the *old* replica by container id (what `rolling-deploy.sh` does),
  never scale down.
- **Postgres host port is `5433`** (the old `deal-flow-matchmaker-postgres-1`
  container still holds `5432`). All host ports are overridable via `.env`.
- **Gateway Dockerfile must not set `NODE_ENV=production`** — Sequelize `sync`
  (its only schema mechanism) is skipped in production.
- `OPENAI_API_KEY` is optional: empty → deterministic feature-hash embeddings, so
  the profile → match flow still works (no semantic embeddings).

## Conventions

- Secrets live in `.env` only (gitignored). Never commit keys, JWT secrets, or the pem.
- Frontend: Vite + React 18 + GSAP. Reuse the design tokens in `frontend/src/styles.css`
  (`--accent`, `--font-ui`/`--font-serif`, `.btn`/`.btn-primary`/`.btn-ghost`) — match
  the existing system, don't introduce a parallel one.
- CSS is global: namespace view-specific classes (e.g. landing uses `vn-lp-*`) to avoid
  collisions with other views' class names.

content.text:
Confirmed — **keep `extract`, it's essential** (removing it would break matching entirely). It's referenced in three places:
1. **gateway** → calls it to enrich a profile on create/update (populates `extracted_profiles`).
2. **ai-data-platform** → fetches the user's profile from it (`GET /extracted/:userId`) on **every** `/matches` request — after the DB split, extract *is* the platform's profile source. Remove it and matches return 502.
3. **frontend** (`/api/agents` → extract) → the extract-then-retry flow.

So it's now more central than before. I won't remove it. Continuing the CLAUDE.md update to capture all this, then commit + push:

input.new_string:
- **Two Postgres, split by concern.** `dqplus-postgres` (host `:5433`, `DB_PORT`) holds
  ONLY user info — `users`/`profiles`/`extracted_profiles` — used by gateway + extract.
  `dqplus-platform-postgres` (host `:5432`, `PLATFORM_DB_PORT`) holds the ai-data-platform
  data — `entities`/`edges`/`matches`/sagas/jobs/…. The platform does **not** read user
  tables by SQL; it fetches profiles from **extract** over HTTP (`EXTRACT_SERVICE_URL`,
  `GET /extracted/:userId`). So `extract` is a hard dependency of matching — don't remove it.
  All host ports overridable via `.env`.
- **Gateway Dockerfile must not set `NODE_ENV=production`** — Sequelize `sync`
  (its only schema mechanism) is skipped in production.
- `OPENAI_API_KEY` is optional: empty → deterministic feature-hash embeddings, so the
  profile → match flow still works (no semantic embeddings). The ai-data-platform talks to
  the **FPT gateway** (`OPENAI_BASE_URL=https://mkp-api.fptcloud.com`) with embedder
  **`multilingual-e5-large` (1024-dim — not OpenAI models)** and needs a browser
  `User-Agent` on every call (FPT is behind Cloudflare, which 403s the default urllib UA).
  Its key/models live in `ai-data-platform/.env` (gitignored), scoped to that service via
  compose `env_file`. Corpus and query embeddings MUST use the same embedder.

- `OPENAI_API_KEY` is optional: empty → deterministic feature-hash embeddings, so the
  profile → match flow still works (no semantic embeddings). The ai-data-platform talks to
  the **FPT gateway** (`OPENAI_BASE_URL=https://mkp-api.fptcloud.com`) with embedder
  **`multilingual-e5-large` (1024-dim — not OpenAI models)** and needs a browser
  `User-Agent` on every call (FPT is behind Cloudflare, which 403s the default urllib UA).
  Its key/models live in `ai-data-platform/.env` (gitignored), scoped to that service via
  compose `env_file`. Corpus and query embeddings MUST use the same embedder.
toolUseResult.originalFile:
# VietNexus — project rules for Claude

Innovation OS matching Vietnamese startups with investors/partners/universities.
Monorepo. Full stack runs in Docker (`docker-compose.yml`). Public site:
**https://dqplus.ddns.net**. Read [`docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`](docs/ARCHITECTURE.md)
before making structural changes.

Matching (semantic **RAG** over a pgvector corpus + relationship **GraphRAG**) is served
by the **ai-data-platform** service (`:8000`); the old `matching-engine` is retired
(disabled via a compose `profiles: ["disabled"]` tag).

## Deployment — read before touching the live site

The public chain is: **VPS nginx → autossh SSH tunnel → local `:5173` → `proxy`
container → `web` replicas**. Only one container can bind a host port, so the
`web` service is internal-only and scaled behind the `proxy`.

- **Deploy a frontend change with `bash deploy/rolling-deploy.sh`.** It rolls a
  new `web` replica in and drains the old one with zero dropped requests. **Never**
  run `docker compose up -d --build web` against the live site — it stops the old
  container before the new one binds (seconds of downtime).
- **`web` must stay scalable**: no `container_name`, no published `ports:` — keep
  `expose: "80"`. The `proxy` service owns the public port (`WEB_PORT`, default 5173).
- **Edit `deploy/proxy.conf` → apply with `docker exec dqplus-proxy nginx -s reload`**
  (zero downtime). Recreating the `proxy` container briefly drops `:5173`.
- **The SSH tunnels** are managed by `deploy/port-forward.sh {start|stop|status}`
  (targets `127.0.0.1`, not the LAN IP — WSL2 mirrored networking makes
  docker-published ports unreachable via the LAN IP). Don't start tunnels by hand.
  `deploy/hackathonai.pem` and `deploy/port-forward.sh` are local-only (gitignored).
- **Public `/api/*` routing is per-service via dedicated reverse tunnels, NOT through
  the `web` nginx.** The VPS nginx maps each prefix to a tunnel port (`port-forward.sh`):
  `/api/backend`→5000→gateway, `/api/agents`→5002→extract,
  `/api/matches`→5003→**ai-data-platform:8000**, `/`→8443→`:5173`→web. So editing
  `frontend/nginx.conf` only changes the LOCAL `:5173` chain — the public `/api/matches`
  path is governed by the VPS nginx + the `5003` tunnel target.

## Gotchas learned the hard way

- **Container healthchecks must hit `127.0.0.1`, not `localhost`.** `localhost`
  resolves to `::1`; the services bind IPv4 only, so `localhost` reports a healthy
  container as unhealthy and hangs `docker compose --wait`.
- **`docker compose --scale web=N` scales *down* by removing the newest replica.**
  To roll, drain the *old* replica by container id (what `rolling-deploy.sh` does),
  never scale down.
- **Postgres host port is `5433`** (the old `deal-flow-matchmaker-postgres-1`
  container still holds `5432`). All host ports are overridable via `.env`.
- **Gateway Dockerfile must not set `NODE_ENV=production`** — Sequelize `sync`
  (its only schema mechanism) is skipped in production.
- `OPENAI_API_KEY` is optional: empty → deterministic feature-hash embeddings, so
  the profile → match flow still works (no semantic embeddings).

## Conventions

- Secrets live in `.env` only (gitignored). Never commit keys, JWT secrets, or the pem.
- Frontend: Vite + React 18 + GSAP. Reuse the design tokens in `frontend/src/styles.css`
  (`--accent`, `--font-ui`/`--font-serif`, `.btn`/`.btn-primary`/`.btn-ghost`) — match
  the existing system, don't introduce a parallel one.
- CSS is global: namespace view-specific classes (e.g. landing uses `vn-lp-*`) to avoid
  collisions with other views' class names.
